chunk_summary: This section outlines a tele-nutrition checklist for managing celiac disease, focusing on dietary adherence, symptom monitoring, growth assessment, and micronutrient risks, with escalation triggers for persistent issues.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of a tele-nutrition checklist for celiac disease management.
Identify key areas for monitoring dietary adherence and gluten exposure in telehealth settings.
Recognize symptoms and growth patterns that may indicate relapse or complications.
Learn escalation criteria for referring patients to pediatric gastroenterology.
key_facts: Celiac disease management requires strict adherence to a lifelong gluten-free diet.
Hidden gluten sources include spices, sauces, processed snacks, and cross-contamination in shared kitchens.
Growth assessment should focus on trajectory and trigger faltering growth pathways if centile thresholds are crossed.
First-line serology includes tTG IgA and total IgA, with interpretation dependent on timing and adherence.
Escalation is needed for persistent symptoms, growth faltering, or anemia unresponsive to therapy.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Celiac disease care depends on strict dietary adherence and structured follow-up, making it suitable for tele-nutrition when escalation rules are explicit.
